description_long: This object consists of two pumpkins, both with a weathered, brownish-orange surface and visible carved features. The larger pumpkin has a jagged, cracked top and a twisted, dark brown stem, with a carved face featuring triangular eyes and a jagged mouth. The smaller pumpkin, positioned beside the larger one, also has a cracked top and a similar stem, with a more distorted face and irregularly shaped eyes and mouth. Both pumpkins have rough, uneven surfaces with visible creases and folds, suggesting age or decay. The interiors are hollow, visible through the carved openings, and the edges of the cutouts are jagged and irregular. The overall appearance is that of traditional jack-o'-lanterns, likely used as seasonal decorations in an urban outdoor setting.
description: A pair of weathered, carved jack-o'-lantern pumpkins with jagged faces and cracked tops, featuring rough, brownish-orange surfaces and twisted stems.
description_view_0: The larger pumpkin is prominent, showing its cracked top, twisted stem, and a partial view of its jagged mouth and eye cutouts.
description_view_1: Both pumpkins are visible from the front, clearly displaying their carved faces with triangular and irregular eyes and mouths.
description_view_2: The side view highlights the depth of the carvings and the cracked tops of both pumpkins, with the smaller pumpkin in the foreground.
description_view_3: The rear and side of both pumpkins are visible, showing mostly uncarved, rough surfaces and the stems, with minimal facial features in view.
attribute_carving_pattern: Traditional jack-o'-lantern style with jagged, irregular cutouts forming eyes and mouths; the faces are expressive and asymmetrical, with no geometric or abstract motifs.
